meaning: adj.磨碎的,臼齿的,质量的,[化][物]摩尔的n.臼齿,磨牙
examples: What we have to do is get back underneath this reaction and see what the molar quantities are.
A over the molar volume squared.
So this is the molar volume of the gas.
What with all this and his toothache (back molar, swollen jaw) and the malaria, G. is in a bad way.
The endemic Siberian population had different molar features and a "very unique DNA signature" - that was dated to be almost 900, 000 years old.
